incidents.attacker: Iran — Ministry of Intelligence (MOIS) hacktivist proxies
incidents.vector: Compromise of the FBI director's personal email account and publication of contents by Iran-linked hacktivist group.
incidents.impact: Personal and confidential emails, documents, and photos exfiltrated and published online. FBI confirmed the targeting but said information was historical and contained no government data.

incidents.attacker: Iran — Ministry of Intelligence (MOIS) hacktivist proxies
incidents.vector: Mass data-wiping attack using destructive malware deployed across the company's global IT infrastructure.
incidents.impact: Over 200,000 systems, servers, and mobile devices reportedly wiped. More than 5,000 workers sent home in Ireland. Offices in 79 countries forced to shut down. Employee devices defaced with attacker's logo.

incidents.vector: TeamPCP compromised the Telnyx Python SDK on PyPI using stolen credentials, injecting credential-stealing payloads hidden via WAV audio file steganography.
incidents.impact: Malicious versions of the SDK (670,000+ monthly downloads) distributed before quarantine. Credential harvesting targeted SSH keys, cloud tokens, and crypto wallets. New steganography TTP observed.

incidents.vector: LAPSUS$ claims breach using credentials stolen during the TeamPCP supply chain campaign, gaining access to internal code repositories and cloud infrastructure.
incidents.impact: Claimed 3GB of data stolen including internal code repos, AWS/Azure/Terraform configs, GitHub Enterprise user data, and employee information. AstraZeneca has not confirmed. Access being sold via encrypted messaging.

incidents.vector: TeamPCP executed cascading supply chain attacks, compromising Trivy and Checkmarx security scanners via stolen credentials, then propagating to LiteLLM and Telnyx PyPI packages.
incidents.impact: Credential-stealing malware injected into widely used open-source security and AI tools. SSH keys, cloud credentials, Kubernetes tokens exfiltrated. TeamPCP partnered with Vect ransomware-as-a-service to arm 300,000 BreachForums users with affiliate keys.

incidents.vector: TeamPCP deployed CanisterWorm, a self-propagating worm targeting exposed Docker APIs, Kubernetes clusters, and Redis servers, with a wiper payload triggered by Iranian timezone/locale settings.
incidents.impact: Wiper destroys data on Kubernetes clusters or local machines if the system matches Iran's timezone or Farsi language settings. Orchestrated via tamperproof blockchain-based canisters resistant to takedown.

incidents.vector: Exploitation of CVE-2026-33017 (CVSS 9.8), a code injection vulnerability in Langflow's public API endpoint enabling arbitrary Python code execution without authentication.
incidents.impact: Over 1,000 exploitation attempts observed across multiple regions within 24 hours of disclosure. API keys for OpenAI, Anthropic, and AWS extracted. CISA added to KEV catalog.

incidents.vector: Three distinct China-aligned threat clusters conducted parallel espionage campaigns using USB-propagated malware, custom backdoors, and novel loaders against a single government target.
incidents.impact: Persistent access established via multiple RATs (PUBLOAD, FluffyGh0st, Gorem, Masol). Keylogging, data theft, and credential harvesting deployed across multiple endpoints. Campaign active since June 2025.

incidents.victim: DOJ dismantles four botnets (3M+ IoT devices)
incidents.vector: Four botnets (Aisuru, Kimwolf, JackSkid, Mossad) compromised over 3 million IoT devices including routers and cameras to launch DDoS attacks and demand extortion payments.
incidents.impact: Over 316,000 combined attack commands launched. Hundreds of thousands of DDoS attacks against targets including DoD. Victims reported tens of thousands of dollars in losses. Infrastructure seized by DOJ.

incidents.vector: Armenian national extradited to US for developing RedLine infostealer malware, which was sold to affiliates via underground forums since 2020 for credential and financial data theft.
incidents.impact: RedLine deployed in thousands of attacks across 150+ countries, extracting login credentials, credit cards, crypto wallets, and VPN logins. Developer faces up to 30 years in prison.

incidents.vector: LeakBase operated as a subscription-based marketplace trading hundreds of millions of compromised records including banking info, credentials, and corporate documents.
incidents.impact: Suspected admin arrested in Russia. FBI and European partners previously conducted 100+ law enforcement actions against 45 individuals across a dozen countries. Forum domains seized.

incidents.vector: Pro-Ukrainian hacktivist group Bearlyfy deployed custom GenieLocker ransomware and variants built on leaked LockBit 3 and Babuk code against Russian businesses.
incidents.impact: More than 70 attacks in the past year, with ransom demands escalating to hundreds of thousands of dollars. Roughly one in five victims pays. Group has begun developing its own malware.

incidents.victim: Users in Saudi Arabia, Turkey, Malaysia, and Ukraine
incidents.attacker: Unknown — commercial surveillance vendors and state actors
incidents.vector: DarkSword iOS exploit chain using six zero-day vulnerabilities in iOS 18.4-18.7, delivered via watering hole campaigns and Snapchat-themed decoy sites.
incidents.impact: Full device compromise deploying GHOSTBLADE, GHOSTKNIFE, and GHOSTSABER malware. Data exfiltration of accounts, messages, browser data, location history, and audio recordings. Multiple state and commercial actors using the same chain.

incidents.vector: Ransomware attack disrupting operations at a major Spanish fishing port.
incidents.impact: Port operations disrupted. Details of the specific ransomware variant and extent of data compromise not yet disclosed.

incidents.vector: Cyber breach affecting internal systems at the Dutch Ministry of Finance, currently under investigation.
incidents.impact: Internal systems compromised. The ministry is probing the extent of the breach. Full scope not yet disclosed.

incidents.vector: Hacker stole data related to customer service tickets from the anime streaming platform.
incidents.impact: Customer service ticket data compromised, potentially including personal information shared in support interactions. Full scope under investigation.

incidents.vector: Cyberattack forcing the government agency to cancel driver's license appointments.
incidents.impact: Driver's license services disrupted across Puerto Rico. Agency systems taken offline as a precaution.

incidents.vector: Tycoon2FA adversary-in-the-middle phishing platform resumed operations after March 4 takedown, using CAPTCHA pages and fake Microsoft 365/Google login portals to steal session cookies and bypass MFA.
incidents.impact: Platform responsible for 62% of phishing attempts blocked by Microsoft in mid-2025 and 30+ million malicious emails in a single month. Operations have continued despite law enforcement disruption.
